education.degree: 1. Chartered Accountant (CA) Role Summary: A Chartered Accountant is responsible for managing accounting, auditing, taxation, and financial advisory services while ensuring compliance with statutory and regulatory requirements. Key Responsibilities: • Prepare and review financial statements as per applicable accounting standards • Manage direct and indirect tax compliance (Income Tax, GST, TDS) • Conduct audits and provide assurance on financial records • Advise management on financial planning, budgeting, and cost control • Ensure compliance with Companies Act, Income Tax Act, and GST laws • Support business decision-making through financial analysis Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified Chartered Accountant (ICAI) • Strong knowledge of accounting standards, taxation, and audit • Analytical and problem-solving skills • Proficiency in accounting software and Excel 2. Practicing Chartered Accountant Role Summary: A Practicing CA provides professional services independently or through a firm, offering audit, taxation, compliance, and advisory services to multiple clients. Key Responsibilities: • Handle statutory, tax, and internal audits for clients • File income tax and GST returns for individuals and businesses • Provide business, financial, and compliance advisory services • Represent clients before tax authorities and regulators • Manage client relationships and ensure service quality • Stay updated with regulatory and legal changes Skills & Qualifications: • CA qualification with Certificate of Practice (COP) • Client handling and communication skills • Strong knowledge of laws and regulations • Business development and advisory capability 3. Audit Manager Role Summary: The Audit Manager leads audit engagements, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and risk mitigation in financial reporting. Key Responsibilities: • Plan and manage statutory, internal, and special audits • Supervise audit teams and review audit work papers • Identify financial and operational risks • Ensure compliance with auditing standards and laws • Prepare audit reports and present findings to management • Coordinate with clients and senior leadership Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Strong audit and risk assessment expertise • Leadership and team management skills • Knowledge of auditing standards and regulatory frameworks 4. Tax Manager Role Summary: The Tax Manager oversees direct and indirect tax planning, compliance, and litigation support to minimize tax risk and ensure regulatory adherence. Key Responsibilities: • Manage income tax, GST, and other statutory tax filings • Develop tax planning strategies to optimize tax liability • Handle tax assessments, audits, and notices • Monitor changes in tax laws and ensure compliance • Coordinate with consultants and tax authorities • Maintain accurate tax documentation and reports Skills & Qualifications: • CA qualification with specialization in taxation • Strong understanding of tax laws and procedures • Analytical and strategic thinking • Attention to detail and compliance focus 5. Assurance Manager Role Summary: The Assurance Manager provides independent assurance on financial and operational processes to enhance reliability and stakeholder confidence. Key Responsibilities: • Manage assurance engagements and client relationships • Evaluate internal controls and compliance frameworks • Review financial statements and disclosures • Ensure adherence to assurance and auditing standards • Identify control gaps and recommend improvements • Prepare assurance reports for stakeholders Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Strong understanding of assurance standards • Risk assessment and analytical skills • Excellent reporting and communication skills 6. Accounting & Compliance Manager Role Summary: Responsible for managing end-to-end accounting operations and ensuring statutory and regulatory compliance. Key Responsibilities: • Oversee accounting processes and monthly closures • Ensure compliance with Companies Act, GST, Income Tax, and other laws • Prepare financial statements and MIS reports • Coordinate with auditors and regulators • Maintain accounting controls and documentation • Lead compliance audits and reviews Skills & Qualifications: • CA qualification • Strong accounting and compliance knowledge • Experience with ERP/accounting systems • Process management and coordination skills 7. Risk & Compliance Manager Role Summary: The Risk & Compliance Manager identifies, assesses, and mitigates financial and regulatory risks within the organization. Key Responsibilities: • Develop and implement risk management frameworks • Monitor regulatory compliance across functions • Conduct compliance audits and risk assessments • Report risk exposure to senior management • Ensure internal controls and governance standards • Support regulatory inspections and audits Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Knowledge of risk management and compliance frameworks • Strong analytical and governance skills • Stakeholder management capability 8. Financial Reporting Manager Role Summary: Responsible for accurate, timely, and compliant financial reporting in line with statutory and accounting standards. Key Responsibilities: • Prepare financial statements as per Ind AS / IFRS • Ensure accurate consolidation and disclosures • Coordinate with auditors during audits • Maintain reporting calendars and compliance timelines • Analyze financial performance and trends • Support management reporting and decision-making Skills & Qualifications: • CA qualification • Strong financial reporting and accounting standards knowledge • High attention to detail • Advanced Excel and reporting tools proficiency 9. Statutory Auditor Role Summary: A Statutory Auditor examines financial statements to provide an independent opinion on their accuracy and legal compliance. Key Responsibilities: • Conduct statutory audits under applicable laws • Verify financial records and supporting documents • Assess compliance with accounting standards and regulations • Identify misstatements and control weaknesses • Prepare audit reports and certifications • Communicate audit findings to stakeholders Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • In-depth auditing and accounting knowledge • Strong professional judgment • Ethical and independent mindset 10. Internal Auditor Role Summary: The Internal Auditor evaluates internal controls, processes, and risk management to improve operational efficiency and governance. Key Responsibilities: • Conduct internal audits across departments • Evaluate internal control effectiveness • Identify operational and financial risks • Recommend process improvements • Prepare audit reports and follow-up actions • Support management in strengthening controls Skills & Qualifications: • CA qualification • Strong internal audit and process review skills • Analytical and investigative mindset • Good communication and reporting skills 11. Concurrent Auditor Role Summary: The Concurrent Auditor performs real-time audits, primarily in banks and financial institutions, to ensure ongoing compliance and accuracy. Key Responsibilities: • Review daily transactions and banking operations • Ensure adherence to RBI and internal guidelines • Detect irregularities and fraud risks • Report exceptions on a timely basis • Support regulatory inspections and audits • Maintain detailed audit documentation Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Knowledge of banking operations and RBI norms • High attention to detail • Strong reporting and observation skills 12. Forensic Auditor Role Summary: A Forensic Auditor investigates financial frauds, disputes, and irregularities using accounting, auditing, and investigative techniques. Key Responsibilities: • Investigate financial frauds and misconduct • Analyze complex financial data and transactions • Collect and document audit evidence • Prepare forensic reports for legal proceedings • Support litigation and regulatory investigations • Coordinate with legal and enforcement agencies Skills & Qualifications: • CA qualification • Expertise in forensic accounting and investigation • Strong analytical and investigative skills • Knowledge of legal and regulatory processes FINANCE & ACCOUNTS ROLES 13. Finance Manager Role Summary: The Finance Manager is responsible for managing the organization’s financial planning, accounting operations, reporting, and compliance to support business objectives. Key Responsibilities: • Oversee day-to-day finance and accounting operations • Prepare budgets, forecasts, and variance analysis • Ensure accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports • Manage statutory compliance (GST, Income Tax, TDS, ROC) • Coordinate with auditors, bankers, and consultants • Support management with financial analysis and decision support Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified Chartered Accountant • Strong knowledge of accounting, taxation, and reporting • Experience with ERP systems and MIS reporting • Analytical and communication skills 14. Senior Finance Manager Role Summary: The Senior Finance Manager leads finance strategy execution, complex reporting, and team management while partnering with senior leadership. Key Responsibilities: • Lead finance team and oversee financial operations • Drive budgeting, forecasting, and long-term financial planning • Review financial statements and management reports • Monitor cash flow, working capital, and cost optimization • Ensure regulatory and statutory compliance • Act as a key business partner to senior management Skills & Qualifications: • CA with significant post-qualification experience • Strong leadership and stakeholder management skills • Advanced financial analysis and strategic planning expertise 15. Head of Finance Role Summary: The Head of Finance is responsible for the overall financial health, governance, and financial strategy of the organization. Key Responsibilities: • Define and implement financial strategy aligned with business goals • Oversee accounting, finance, taxation, treasury, and compliance • Lead budgeting, forecasting, and performance management • Ensure strong internal controls and governance frameworks • Support strategic initiatives such as expansions, investments, and funding • Report financial performance to top management and board Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Strong strategic, leadership, and decision-making capabilities • Deep understanding of corporate finance and compliance 16. Financial Controller (FC) Role Summary: The Financial Controller ensures accurate financial reporting, robust internal controls, and compliance with accounting standards and laws. Key Responsibilities: • Oversee accounting records and financial statements • Ensure compliance with Ind AS / IFRS and statutory requirements • Manage audits and liaison with statutory auditors • Implement and monitor internal financial controls • Oversee closing processes and reporting timelines • Support management with financial insights and risk analysis Skills & Qualifications: • CA with strong financial reporting background • Expertise in accounting standards and controls • High attention to detail and governance focus 17. Group Financial Controller Role Summary: The Group Financial Controller manages consolidated financial reporting and controls across multiple entities or group companies. Key Responsibilities: • Oversee group-level accounting and consolidation • Prepare consolidated financial statements • Ensure consistency in accounting policies across entities • Manage group audits and regulatory reporting • Strengthen group-wide internal controls and governance • Support group leadership with financial insights Skills & Qualifications: • CA with consolidation and group reporting experience • Strong knowledge of Ind AS / IFRS • Ability to manage multi-entity structures 18. Accounts Manager Role Summary: The Accounts Manager manages daily accounting operations, ensuring accurate records and timely reporting. Key Responsibilities: • Supervise accounting entries and ledger maintenance • Manage accounts payable and receivable • Ensure timely bank reconciliations • Support month-end and year-end closures • Assist in statutory compliance and audits • Maintain accounting documentation and controls Skills & Qualifications: • CA with hands-on accounting experience • Strong accounting principles knowledge • ERP and Excel proficiency 19. Senior Accounts Manager Role Summary: The Senior Accounts Manager oversees accounting teams and complex accounting issues while ensuring compliance and accuracy. Key Responsibilities: • Lead accounting team and review accounting records • Handle complex accounting transactions • Ensure compliance with accounting standards and laws • Coordinate with auditors and tax consultants • Prepare MIS and management reports • Strengthen accounting controls and processes Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Strong technical accounting and team leadership skills 20. Chief Accountant Role Summary: The Chief Accountant is responsible for maintaining accurate financial records and ensuring compliance with accounting standards and internal policies. Key Responsibilities: • Oversee general ledger and financial records • Ensure compliance with accounting standards and company policies • Supervise accounting staff and processes • Support financial reporting and audits • Maintain internal controls over accounting activities • Assist senior management in financial reviews Skills & Qualifications: • CA qualification • Strong accounting and control background LEADERSHIP & CXO ROLES 21. Chief Financial Officer (CFO) Role Summary: The CFO is responsible for the overall financial strategy, performance, governance, and long￾term financial sustainability of the organization. Key Responsibilities: • Lead financial strategy and planning • Oversee finance, accounting, taxation, treasury, and compliance • Manage capital structure, funding, and investor relations • Drive profitability, cost management, and cash flow optimization • Support M&A, strategic investments, and growth initiatives • Present financial performance to board and stakeholders Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA with extensive leadership experience • Strong strategic, financial, and business acumen • Excellent stakeholder and board-level communication skills 22. Deputy CFO Role Summary: The Deputy CFO supports the CFO in managing finance functions and ensures smooth execution of financial strategies. Key Responsibilities: • Assist CFO in financial planning and strategy execution • Oversee financial operations and reporting • Manage finance teams and cross-functional coordination • Ensure compliance and internal controls • Step in as acting CFO when required • Support board and investor reporting Skills & Qualifications: • CA with senior leadership experience • Strong operational and strategic finance skills 23. Finance Director Role Summary: The Finance Director focuses on governance, compliance, and financial leadership at the board level. Key Responsibilities: • Provide strategic financial guidance to the board • Ensure corporate governance and regulatory compliance • Oversee financial reporting and audits • Evaluate major investments and strategic decisions • Strengthen risk management and control frameworks • Represent finance function at executive level Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Board-level finance and governance experience 24. Executive Director - Finance Role Summary: The Executive Director - Finance oversees finance operations at the highest executive level and contributes to organizational strategy. Key Responsibilities: • Lead finance function across the organization • Drive financial strategy and performance management • Oversee compliance, reporting, and governance • Support corporate restructuring, M&A, and growth plans • Work closely with board and top leadership Skills & Qualifications: • CA with extensive executive experience • Strong leadership and strategic thinking 25. Vice President - Finance Role Summary: The VP - Finance bridges strategic planning and operational execution, supporting organizational growth and financial discipline. Key Responsibilities: • Lead financial planning and performance management • Oversee finance teams and reporting processes • Support fundraising, investor relations, and strategic initiatives • Ensure robust financial controls and compliance • Partner with business heads for financial decision-making Skills & Qualifications: • CA with senior management experience • Strong analytical, leadership, and communication skills 26. Chief Accounting Officer (CAO) Role Summary: The CAO is responsible for all accounting policies, financial reporting accuracy, and compliance with accounting standards. Key Responsibilities: • Define and maintain accounting policies and standards • Ensure accurate financial reporting and disclosures • Oversee accounting operations and teams • Manage statutory and internal audits • Ensure compliance with Ind AS / IFRS / GAAP • Support CFO and board on accounting matters Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Deep expertise in accounting standards and reporting • Strong governance and control orientation TAXATION ROLES (DIRECT & INDIRECT TAX) 27. Tax Consultant Role Summary: The Tax Consultant provides advisory and compliance support in direct and indirect taxes, ensuring clients or the organization remain compliant while optimizing tax positions. Key Responsibilities: • Prepare and review Income Tax and GST returns • Handle tax computations for individuals and businesses • Assist in tax planning and advisory assignments • Support tax audits, assessments, and notices • Maintain tax documentation and working papers • Stay updated with changes in tax laws and regulations Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified Chartered Accountant • Good understanding of Income Tax and GST laws • Strong analytical and documentation skills • Client communication and advisory ability 28. Senior Tax Consultant Role Summary: The Senior Tax Consultant manages complex tax matters, supervises junior consultants, and provides advanced tax advisory support. Key Responsibilities: • Review tax computations and filings • Handle complex tax planning and advisory cases • Manage tax assessments, appeals, and notices • Supervise and mentor junior tax team members • Coordinate with clients and tax authorities • Ensure high-quality tax deliverables Skills & Qualifications: • CA with strong post-qualification tax experience • In-depth knowledge of tax laws and interpretations • Leadership and client-handling skills 29. Direct Tax Manager Role Summary: The Direct Tax Manager oversees direct tax compliance, planning, and litigation support to manage tax risks effectively. Key Responsibilities: • Manage corporate income tax compliance and filings • Develop and implement tax planning strategies • Handle tax assessments, scrutiny, and appeals • Review advance tax and withholding tax calculations • Monitor changes in tax laws and assess impact • Coordinate with external tax advisors and auditors Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Strong expertise in Income Tax Act and corporate taxation • Strategic tax planning and risk management skills 30. Indirect Tax Manager Role Summary: The Indirect Tax Manager ensures compliance with indirect tax laws and optimizes tax processes and structures. Key Responsibilities: • Oversee GST compliance and return filings • Manage GST audits, notices, and assessments • Advise on GST implications of transactions • Ensure correct tax classification and credit utilization • Monitor changes in indirect tax laws • Coordinate with tax authorities and consultants Skills & Qualifications: • CA with specialization in indirect taxation • In-depth GST and indirect tax knowledge • Strong compliance and advisory skills 31. GST Consultant Role Summary: The GST Consultant provides compliance, advisory, and representation services related to Goods and Services Tax. Key Responsibilities: • Prepare and file GST returns and reconciliations • Advise on GST applicability and classification • Handle GST notices, audits, and assessments • Assist in GST registrations and amendments • Reconcile GST data with books and returns • Ensure compliance with GST laws and timelines Skills & Qualifications: • Chartered Accountant • Strong GST law and compliance knowledge • Attention to detail and analytical skills 32. GST Manager Role Summary: The GST Manager leads GST compliance and advisory functions while managing teams and complex GST matters. Key Responsibilities: • Oversee end-to-end GST compliance • Review GST returns, reconciliations, and reports • Handle complex GST advisory and structuring • Manage GST audits and litigation support • Lead and mentor GST compliance teams • Liaise with tax authorities and senior management Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Advanced expertise in GST and indirect taxes • Team leadership and stakeholder management skills 33. International Tax Consultant Role Summary: The International Tax Consultant advises on cross-border taxation, double taxation avoidance, and global tax compliance. Key Responsibilities: • Advise on international tax structures and transactions • Analyze DTAA, PE exposure, and withholding taxes • Support cross-border tax compliance and reporting • Assist in international tax audits and assessments • Coordinate with global tax teams and advisors • Monitor global tax law developments Skills & Qualifications: • CA with international tax exposure • Strong knowledge of DTAA, OECD guidelines, BEPS • Analytical and research-oriented mindset 34. Transfer Pricing Consultant Role Summary: The Transfer Pricing Consultant ensures compliance with transfer pricing regulations for related-party transactions. Key Responsibilities: • Prepare transfer pricing documentation and reports • Conduct benchmarking and economic analysis • Advise on pricing policies for intercompany transactions • Handle transfer pricing audits and disputes • Coordinate with international teams and advisors • Monitor changes in transfer pricing regulations Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Strong understanding of transfer pricing laws and methods • Data analysis and documentation skills 35. Corporate Tax Advisor Role Summary: The Corporate Tax Advisor provides strategic tax advice to optimize tax structures and ensure compliance. Key Responsibilities: • Advise on corporate tax structuring and planning • Support mergers, acquisitions, and restructuring from a tax perspective • Evaluate tax risks and compliance gaps • Provide opinions on complex tax issues • Coordinate with internal teams and external advisors • Support management in strategic tax decisions Skills & Qualifications: • CA with strong corporate tax advisory experience • Strategic thinking and risk assessment skills • Strong business understanding 36. Tax Litigation Manager Role Summary: The Tax Litigation Manager manages tax disputes, appeals, and legal proceedings before tax authorities and tribunals. Key Responsibilities: • Handle tax litigation and dispute resolution • Prepare submissions, appeals, and legal documentation • Represent organization before tax authorities and tribunals • Coordinate with legal counsels and consultants • Track litigation status and risk exposure • Advise management on litigation strategy and outcomes Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Strong experience in tax litigation and representation • Legal drafting and analytical skills AUDIT & ASSURANCE ROLES 37. Statutory Audit Manager Role Summary: The Statutory Audit Manager leads statutory audit engagements and ensures that financial statements comply with applicable laws and accounting standards. Key Responsibilities: • Plan and manage statutory audits under Companies Act and other laws • Supervise audit teams and review audit working papers • Ensure compliance with Ind AS / IFRS and auditing standards • Identify audit risks and control weaknesses • Finalize audit reports and audit opinions • Liaise with clients, management, and auditors Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified Chartered Accountant • Strong statutory audit and reporting experience • Leadership and client-handling skills 38. Internal Audit Manager Role Summary: The Internal Audit Manager evaluates internal controls, risk management, and governance processes to improve operational efficiency. Key Responsibilities: • Develop and execute internal audit plans • Lead internal audit assignments across functions • Evaluate internal controls and risk management systems • Identify process gaps and recommend improvements • Prepare internal audit reports and follow-up actions • Coordinate with management and audit committees Skills & Qualifications: • CA with strong internal audit experience • Knowledge of risk-based auditing • Strong analytical and communication skills 39. External Auditor Role Summary: The External Auditor provides independent assurance on the accuracy and fairness of financial statements. Key Responsibilities: • Conduct statutory and external audits • Verify financial records and supporting documents • Test internal controls and compliance • Identify misstatements and audit risks • Prepare audit documentation and reports • Communicate audit findings to stakeholders Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified Chartered Accountant • Strong accounting and auditing standards knowledge • Professional skepticism and integrity 40. Concurrent Auditor (Banking) Role Summary: The Concurrent Auditor performs real-time audits of banking operations to ensure compliance with RBI guidelines and internal policies. Key Responsibilities: • Review daily banking transactions and operations • Ensure adherence to RBI norms and bank policies • Identify irregularities, frauds, and control lapses • Report exceptions on a timely basis • Support RBI inspections and statutory audits • Maintain detailed audit documentation Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Strong knowledge of banking operations and RBI regulations • High attention to detail 41. Systems Auditor Role Summary: The Systems Auditor evaluates IT systems, controls, and processes to ensure data integrity, security, and operational effectiveness. Key Responsibilities: • Audit IT systems and application controls • Review access controls, data security, and system integrity • Evaluate IT general controls and automated processes • Assess system risks and recommend improvements • Coordinate with IT teams and management • Prepare system audit reports Skills & Qualifications: • CA with systems audit experience • Knowledge of IT controls and ERP systems • Analytical and technical mindset 42. SOX Auditor Role Summary: The SOX Auditor ensures compliance with the Sarbanes-Oxley Act by evaluating internal controls over financial reporting. Key Responsibilities: • Test internal controls under SOX framework • Document control design and operating effectiveness • Identify control deficiencies and remediation plans • Coordinate with process owners and management • Support internal and external SOX audits • Maintain SOX documentation and compliance reports Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Strong understanding of SOX and ICFR • Process documentation and testing expertise 43. Process Auditor Role Summary: The Process Auditor reviews business processes to identify inefficiencies, risks, and control gaps. Key Responsibilities: • Conduct process audits across departments • Map processes and identify improvement areas • Evaluate operational controls and SOP adherence • Recommend process enhancements and efficiencies • Prepare process audit reports • Follow up on implementation of recommendations Skills & Qualifications: • CA with process audit experience • Strong process analysis and documentation skills 44. Compliance Auditor Role Summary: The Compliance Auditor ensures adherence to regulatory, statutory, and internal compliance requirements. Key Responsibilities: • Conduct compliance audits and reviews • Verify adherence to laws, regulations, and internal policies • Identify compliance risks and violations • Prepare compliance audit reports • Support regulatory inspections and audits • Advise management on compliance improvements Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Strong regulatory and compliance knowledge • Attention to detail and governance focus 45. Quality Review Auditor Role Summary: The Quality Review Auditor evaluates audit quality, methodologies, and compliance with professional standards. Key Responsibilities: • Review audit files for quality and standard compliance • Assess adherence to auditing standards and firm policies • Identify gaps in audit execution and documentation • Provide feedback and recommendations to audit teams • Support audit quality improvement initiatives • Assist in regulatory and peer reviews Skills & Qualifications: • CA with strong audit and quality review experience • In-depth knowledge of auditing standards • Strong evaluation and reporting skills FORENSIC, RISK & COMPLIANCE ROLES 46. Forensic Accountant Role Summary: The Forensic Accountant investigates financial irregularities, frauds, and disputes by analyzing financial records and evidence. Key Responsibilities: • Analyze financial data to detect fraud and irregularities • Conduct forensic accounting investigations • Examine transactions, books, and supporting documents • Prepare forensic reports and documentation • Support legal proceedings and regulatory investigations • Coordinate with legal teams and enforcement agencies Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified Chartered Accountant • Strong forensic accounting and analytical skills • Attention to detail and investigative mindset 47. Forensic Audit Manager Role Summary: The Forensic Audit Manager leads forensic investigations and manages teams handling complex fraud cases. Key Responsibilities: • Lead forensic audit assignments and investigations • Design investigation approach and scope • Supervise forensic audit teams and review findings • Identify fraud risks and control weaknesses • Prepare forensic reports for management and legal use • Liaise with legal counsels, regulators, and senior management Skills & Qualifications: • CA with strong forensic audit experience • Leadership and investigation management skills • Knowledge of legal and regulatory processes 48. Fraud Risk Analyst Role Summary: The Fraud Risk Analyst identifies, assesses, and mitigates fraud risks within business operations. Key Responsibilities: • Analyze transactions and data for fraud indicators • Conduct fraud risk assessments and monitoring • Develop fraud detection and prevention frameworks • Investigate suspected fraud cases • Prepare fraud risk reports and dashboards • Recommend fraud control and prevention measures Skills & Qualifications: • Chartered Accountant • Strong data analysis and risk assessment skills • Knowledge of fraud prevention techniques 49. Risk Advisory Consultant Role Summary: The Risk Advisory Consultant provides advisory services on risk management, internal controls, and governance frameworks. Key Responsibilities: • Assess business, financial, and operational risks • Evaluate internal control systems and processes • Advise clients on risk mitigation strategies • Support risk transformation and compliance projects • Prepare risk assessment reports and presentations • Coordinate with clients and stakeholders Skills & Qualifications: • CA with risk advisory experience • Strong analytical and consulting skills • Knowledge of enterprise risk frameworks 50. Enterprise Risk Manager (ERM) Role Summary: The Enterprise Risk Manager designs and implements enterprise-wide risk management frameworks to support organizational objectives. Key Responsibilities: • Develop and maintain ERM frameworks • Identify and assess strategic, operational, and financial risks • Monitor risk exposure and mitigation plans • Report risk metrics to senior management and board • Integrate risk management into business strategy • Support regulatory and governance requirements Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified CA • Strong knowledge of ERM frameworks (COSO, ISO) • Strategic thinking and governance skills 51. Compliance Officer Role Summary: The Compliance Officer ensures adherence to regulatory, statutory, and internal compliance requirements. Key Responsibilities: • Monitor compliance with laws and regulations • Develop and update compliance policies and procedures • Conduct compliance audits and reviews • Handle regulatory filings and disclosures • Support regulatory inspections and audits • Train employees on compliance requirements Skills & Qualifications: • Chartered Accountant • Strong regulatory and compliance knowledge • Attention to detail and governance orientation 52. Corporate Governance Manager Role Summary: The Corporate Governance Manager ensures effective governance practices, board compliance, and ethical standards within the organization. Key Responsibilities: • Ensure compliance with corporate governance requirements • Support board and committee operations • Monitor adherence to Companies Act and SEBI regulations • Develop governance policies and frameworks • Coordinate board reporting and disclosures • Advise management on governance best practices Skills & Qualifications: • CA with strong governance and compliance experience • Knowledge of corporate laws and regulations • Strong coordination and communication skills 53. AML / KYC Specialist Role Summary: The AML / KYC Specialist ensures compliance with anti-money laundering and know-your￾customer regulations. Key Responsibilities: • Conduct customer due diligence and KYC reviews • Monitor transactions for AML red flags • Investigate suspicious transactions and activities • File regulatory reports (STR, CTR) • Ensure compliance with RBI and AML regulations • Support AML audits and regulatory inspections Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified Chartered Accountant • Knowledge of AML / KYC laws and frameworks • Strong analytical and investigative skills BANKING & FINANCIAL SERVICES ROLES 54. Banking Accountant Role Summary: The Banking Accountant manages accounting, reconciliation, and regulatory reporting for banking and financial operations. Key Responsibilities: • Maintain accurate accounting records for banking transactions • Perform daily bank reconciliations and ledger reviews • Prepare financial statements and regulatory reports • Ensure compliance with RBI guidelines and banking regulations • Support statutory, internal, and concurrent audits • Coordinate with branches and operations teams Skills & Qualifications: • Qualified Chartered Accountant • Strong knowledge of banking accounting and regulations • Attention to detail and compliance focus 55.
